nodes.label: AI as mentor substitute for knowledge gaps
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable learning tool due to errors
nodes.grounding_span: I never really had a mentor to teach me

nodes.label: AI as task delegator for repetitive work
nodes.label_negative: AI as partner for collaborative debugging
nodes.grounding_span: I delegate the entire task usually but then work through the bugs with it

nodes.label: AI reliability and consistency
nodes.grounding_span: It's common to have small bugs or inconsistencies in the test cases

nodes.label: AI as tool for enjoyable creative tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as tool for boring repetitive tasks
nodes.grounding_span: unless it's creating functionality that I find fun

nodes.label: Workplace AI tool restrictions
nodes.grounding_span: certain AI models are blocked, so that's a bit annoying

nodes.label: AI as skill that matters for future work
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to human skills
nodes.grounding_span: working with AI to get through issues will become a skill that is needed

nodes.label: Learning and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: it's better for my learning and understanding to write it myself

nodes.label: Efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: if I can take a shortcut I usually will

nodes.label: Enjoyment and engagement in work
nodes.grounding_span: creating functionality that I find fun

nodes.label: Professional autonomy and tool choice
nodes.grounding_span: They want us to use Copilot, but I find it to be inferior

nodes.label: Positive satisfaction when AI works on first try
nodes.grounding_span: any time that it's able to nail a task on the first try is honestly very nice

nodes.label: Frustration with workplace AI restrictions
nodes.grounding_span: certain AI models are blocked, so that's a bit annoying

nodes.label: Ambivalence about AI taking over skills
nodes.grounding_span: I worry about AI taking over quite a lot, but its aptitude for certain areas does make me feel like not being good at those areas maybe doesn't matter too much

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI as necessary skill
nodes.grounding_span: I think refusing to use AI is hamstringing yourself

nodes.label: Empirical verification through testing
nodes.grounding_span: I test on my own by moving it around and re-running the tests

nodes.label: Lazy shortcut-seeking tendency
nodes.grounding_span: I'm kind of lazy, so that might not be that way for everybody
